page: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Daldv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a 236 GB disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU scoring 3079.18 and Geekbench single-core reaching 2078.0, both placing in the top 25%. Geekbench text processing also demonstrates strong performance at 2566.0. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present notable constraints. Multi-core stress-ng at 6151.67 and Geekbench multi-core at 4740.0 fall into the average category. Memory bandwidth is weak, with read-only large block speeds at 37.74 GB/sec and small block cached speeds at 303.96 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also weak, with Redis achieving 5.88 million operations per second. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to poor levels for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4alds_v6 offers a low-memory hardware profile that reduces overhead for workloads that do not require large RAM footprints. The inclusion of 236 GB of local storage provides built-in space without requiring external disk attachments. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and weak memory bandwidth represent clear tradeoffs, making this instance less appropriate for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data compilation. The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make this instance appropriate for single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight containerized applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation but have modest memory requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Daldv6 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, a 236 GB local storage disk, and supports Premium Storage. The instance exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its top-tier Geekbench and stress-ng single-core scores. However, it is constrained by weak memory bandwidth and below-average multi-threaded scaling, with database and Redis operations scoring in the lower percentiles. This low-memory profile offers a cost-efficient option for workloads that prioritize raw single-threaded CPU speed over memory capacity. It is appropriate for lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic compute tasks that do not require extensive memory or high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, offering strong single-core performance with low memory density.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an AMD-powered virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, optimized for low-memory compute tasks.
tagline: A low-memory, compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and integrated local storage for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 236 GB of local storage.
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance.
Constrained by weak memory bandwidth and lower database transaction speeds.
Appropriate for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded compute tasks.
